hard.next_step: 6/29/26 GK: pushing opp out, extended silence. will continue to put touches in
hard.ais_why: Scott Fields sized the ANA (Anna autonomous negotiation) pricing as a discrete line item on the 24 Mar 2026 commercial call; the AI module reached the budget conversation. AI importance rated Important, demo reaction 'really good'. Greenfield function, budget-constrained, may start with analytics before adding Anna.

north_star_verdict.headline: Cold ~82 days since Scott's last reply (15 Apr recording request); unfunded greenfield project with no budget owner engaged; close just pushed 60 days to 22 Sep amid extended silence.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-02-09 (~147 days old), in Formal Evaluation since creation with no stage progression. Last genuine two-way buyer touch: Scott's 15 Apr email asking for demo recordings; last live meeting the 24 Mar demo. Rep logged eight-plus follow-ups from 30 Apr through 29 Jun with no buyer reply. Close date pushed 23 Jul to 22 Sep on 30 Jun ('pushing opp out, extended silence'). Cold ~82 days exceeds the 60-day threshold, forcing Off Track; reaching a 22 Sep close would require re-engagement, a funded budget, and contracting from a standing start, which is not credible without buyer re-engagement first.
evidence: Last buyer engagement: Scott Fields email 15 Apr 2026 asking for demo recordings to share with a new PDI hire; last live meeting the 24 Mar 2026 demo.
Scott on 18 Feb discovery: 'this is an unfunded project that I'm gonna have to go beg for.' Budget anchored at ~$60-70k for a one-year modular entry.
Next Step 27 Mar: 'looping in VP for next call to assess budget range' - that budget/VP call never occurred (no Avoma record, no confirmation).
No economic buyer / budget owner engaged on any call or activity; Scott is a solo procurement leader ('I'm solo right now', 24 Mar).
Eight-plus rep follow-ups with no buyer reply: 30 Apr, 05 May, 20 May, 26 May, 15 Jun, 22 Jun (webinar invite), 29 Jun (extended-silence note).
Close date pushed 23 Jul to 22 Sep on 30 Jun by Grace Kim citing extended silence.
No contracting, legal, InfoSec, or order-form activity recorded.

metrics.narrative: Scott Fields is standing up a greenfield procurement function at PDI where NetSuite is used only to pay bills, only about 30 percent of contracts sit in the repository, and the rest is scattered across spreadsheets (18 Feb discovery, Scott). He sized the opportunity on that call: total spend around $100M, procurement-influenceable spend around $60M, and tail spend roughly $12-15M that is unnegotiated and ad hoc. His stated goal is vendor consolidation, demand aggregation, and supplier rationalization, plus autonomous tail-spend negotiation that would 'pay for the platform in the first year' (24 Mar demo, Scott). What is missing is a quantified ROI: no target savings figure, no baseline cycle time or error rate, so the value case is directional, not numeric. Converting his sized $12-15M tail spend into a defensible year-one savings number is the move that would give Scott the ROI he needs to fund the project.
sources: SF Next_Step 2026-02-19 'scope largely Spend / Supplier with limited budget'

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is genuinely unmapped. Scott Fields is the Salesforce Decision Maker (VP and Head of Procurement) but explicitly does NOT control the money: 'this is an unfunded project that I'm gonna have to go beg for' (18 Feb). He referenced a manager who told him to pivot from build to buy ('my boss told me maybe we could pivot', 09 Feb) and a plan to loop a VP in to assess budget range (Next Step 27 Mar), but that budget conversation never happened and no budget owner has ever been named or engaged. No CFO, COO, or finance sponsor appears in any call, task, or contact role. The single highest-leverage MEDDPICC move is to get Scott to name and introduce the person who can approve the ~$60-70k spend.
sources: SF Next_Step 2026-03-27 ('looping in VP to assess budget range')
SF OpportunityContactRole (Scott Fields = Decision Maker, only role on file)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are clear from the 24 Mar demo and 18 Feb discovery: (1) AI auto-classification that turns messy NetSuite and spreadsheet data into analysis-ready spend (AutoClass), (2) actionable consolidation/optimization insight, not just charts - Scott pushed back that 'top 5 spend I can get from a spreadsheet... what I'm looking for is my top 5 vendor consolidation opportunities', (3) Microsoft Teams-embedded conversational access (Merlin Insights, since PDI runs on Teams), (4) autonomous tail-spend negotiation (Anna), and (5) a modular, low-cost, phaseable entry with no forced full-suite purchase. Scott confirmed 'demand aggregation and supplier rationalization will probably be the first two' (24 Mar). He also flagged the contract-compliance criterion is capped until he loads more contracts (only 30 percent are in the system). Criteria are confirmed for the analytics module; the weighting between analytics-first and Anna is still budget-dependent.

decision_process.narrative: Read from the call sequence, the process is a single-threaded, informal evaluation run entirely by Scott: intro (09 Feb) then discovery (18 Feb) then demo (24 Mar) then quote (06 Apr), with an intended budget/VP step (Next Step 27 Mar) that never fired. There is no RFP, no formal committee, and no procurement gauntlet - Scott is the sole evaluator building the function from scratch. The approval chain runs through Scott to an unnamed budget owner he must 'beg' for funding; he owns internal financial and risk approvals for purchases via the NetSuite intake he built, but this software purchase itself needs money he does not yet hold. Mapping the funding-approval path and adding a second thread are the process gaps that block the next gate.

paper_process.narrative: No paper process has started and none is visible. As a one-to-two module analytics deal (not a full suite), contracting would be an Order Form plus a light SOW rather than a full MSA, so weight it accordingly. PDI's CLM is LinkSquares with signature in DocuSign (09 Feb), and legal/InfoSec routing exists inside Scott's own NetSuite intake workflow, but nothing has been triggered for a Zycus purchase: no redlines, no security questionnaire, no procurement registration. Visibility here only opens once budget is confirmed and a phased order form is put in front of Scott, at which point the LinkSquares/DocuSign path and any InfoSec review can be scoped.
sources: no contracting activity in SF field history or tasks

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is well articulated and owned by Scott Fields. PDI had no procurement function before he arrived; NetSuite 'was used basically just to pay bills', commodity data, start/end dates and renewal tracking were never captured, only 30 percent of contracts are in the repository, and vendor data is scattered across spreadsheets (18 Feb and 09 Feb). Scott is solo with no near-term team ('I'm solo right now. I'm not gonna have a team anytime soon', 24 Mar), so he needs automation that finds consolidation and tail-spend savings without manual effort - 'throw all that stuff into your tool' and 'have at it... pay for the platform in the first year'. The compelling driver is his own need to prove value quickly in a new role: 'anything that I can implement that could bring some value quickly to show some savings will be helpful' (24 Mar). Confirmed pain, clear owner, but no compelling event with a hard date.

champion.narrative: Scott Fields is the only engaged contact and showed early champion behaviour: he attended the Atlanta Zycus event, drove a positive demo on 24 Mar ('this is really good'), asked for a full proposal with standalone and combined pricing options, and looped a newly hired PDI team member onto the demo recording on 15 Apr - a signal of intent to widen the evaluation. What has developed is genuine product interest and a pricing request; what is still missing is decisive: he has no budget authority (must 'beg' for funds), has brought no internal sponsor onto a call, and has gone silent for roughly 82 days across eight-plus rep follow-ups. He is a cooling, at-risk champion, not a mobilised one, and there is no backup thread if he disengages.
sources: SF Task 2026-04-15 (Scott requests demo recordings for new hire)
SF OpportunityContactRole (Scott Fields = Decision Maker, Primary)

competition.narrative: There is no active bake-off. Scott surveyed the market before engaging and named SpendIQ, Zip, Symphony and Supplier.io as spend-analytics tools he had looked at, using their roughly $60-70k module pricing as his budget anchor (09 Feb and 18 Feb) - awareness and price benchmarks, not a confirmed evaluation. Salesforce logs the competitor generically as 'Others'. The real competitor on this deal is no-decision / status quo: an unfunded greenfield project, a solo buyer, and 82 days of silence. We win not by out-featuring a rival but by making the entry cheap and phaseable and by handing Scott a year-one tail-spend ROI he can take to his budget owner.
sources: Avoma intro 2026-02-09 (Scott, 'spend IQs of the world, Zip, Symphony')

deal_movement.summary: Deal peaked at the 24 Mar demo and 06 Apr quote, then stalled. The last genuine buyer touch was Scott's 15 Apr recording request; since then eight-plus rep follow-ups drew no reply, and on 30 Jun the close date was pushed 60 days to 22 Sep on an explicit extended-silence note.

competitive_position.summary: The strongest current threat is no-decision, not a vendor: as of 30 Jun the project is unfunded, single-threaded, and cold 82 days, so the deal is losing to inertia. SpendIQ, Zip, Symphony and Supplier.io were named on 09-18 Feb only as market awareness and a $60-70k price benchmark, never as an active evaluation - all dormant. Salesforce logs 'Others'. We displace do-nothing with a low-cost, phased analytics entry and a fundable year-one tail-spend ROI.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a cold, unfunded, single-threaded early-stage deal; the win path is re-opening the relationship with a budget-fit offer, arming Scott with ROI, and adding a second thread.
flags: Re-open the cold relationship with a phased, analytics-only entry sized to Scott's $60-70k budget - the only credible path on an unfunded greenfield project, else it dies a no-decision.
Arm Scott to 'beg' for budget: convert his sized $12-15M tail spend into a year-one savings number he can take to his funding approver.
Break single-thread on a solo, silent champion: engage the new PDI hire looped in on 15 Apr and get Scott to name the budget owner.

scope_change.from: Intake + eProcurement + spend analytics (Scott's initial Feb '26 priority)
scope_change.to: Spend analytics only (iAnalyze / AutoClass / Merlin Insights); Anna tail-spend and broader suite deferred
scope_change.detail: Budget-driven phasing: Scott capped a ~$60-70k one-year entry, built intake himself in NetSuite, and said start with analytics before adding Anna (18 Feb / 24 Mar). Defensive narrowing typical of an unfunded greenfield buyer wanting a low-risk, phased start.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A greenfield, unfunded spend-analytics deal that stalled: Scott Fields loved the 24 Mar demo but has been silent ~82 days, the close was just pushed 60 days to 22 Sep, and no budget owner is engaged - the fight is against no-decision, not a rival.

win_position.text: Win sits low because a single Formal-Evaluation contact (Scott Fields, VP Procurement) liked the 24 Mar demo and asked for options, but the project is unfunded and cold, holding confidence well below a live, budget-backed deal.
Scott named the blocker himself on 18 Feb: 'this is an unfunded project that I'm gonna have to go beg for' - and no budget owner has ever been engaged.
The buyer has been silent ~82 days since his 15 Apr recording request, and the rep pushed the close from 23 Jul to 22 Sep on 30 Jun citing extended silence.
Genuine fit signal: Scott called the AutoClass analytics demo 'really good' and wants Anna to 'pay for the platform in the first year' (24 Mar).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum stalled after the 24 Mar demo and 06 Apr quote - eight-plus rep touches through 29 Jun drew no buyer reply.
The close date slipped 60 days (23 Jul to 22 Sep) on 30 Jun with the note 'pushing opp out, extended silence'.
Commercials did enter the conversation: standalone analytics, standalone Anna, and combined pricing were quoted on 06 Apr.

customer_commitment.text: No forward commitment: the customer named no date, requested no next meeting, and the intended VP budget call (Next Step 27 Mar) never happened.
Scott asked for a full proposal with options on 24 Mar and looped a new PDI hire onto the demo recording on 15 Apr - intent signals, not commitments.
Single-threaded on a solo procurement leader who said 'I'm solo right now, I'm not gonna have a team anytime soon' (24 Mar).

deal_risk.text: Primary risk is no-decision: an unfunded greenfield project with budget that must be 'begged' for and no economic buyer engaged.
Scope is narrowing defensively to analytics-only with Anna deferred, a budget-driven phasing signal.
Contract-compliance value stays capped until Scott loads contracts - only 30 percent are in the repository (24 Mar).

deal_momentum.evidence: Eight-plus unanswered rep follow-ups and a 60-day close slip on extended silence (30 Jun).

customer_commitment.evidence: No customer-named date, no next meeting requested, single-threaded solo buyer.

deal_risk.evidence: Unfunded project, no economic buyer engaged, cold ~82 days - dominant no-decision risk.

customer_preference.evidence: Positive on the 24 Mar demo ('this is really good') and asked for a proposal with options, but has gone silent ~82 days, is unfunded, and gave no Zycus-specific commitment - leaning toward no-decision.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain plus spend sizing (total ~$100M, influenceable ~$60M, tail ~$12-15M) but no quantified savings or ROI target; 'pay for the platform in the first year' is aspirational.

critical_signals.text: The real threat is no-decision: an unfunded greenfield project Scott must beg budget for, silent since mid-April, with no rival actively in play.
The 24 Mar demo landed well and Scott asked for a full proposal with options, but he has not replied to anything since his 15 Apr recording request.
Pricing is the crux: Scott anchored a $60-70k one-year budget and needs a year-one ROI story to fund it; combined options were quoted 06 Apr with no response.
A new PDI hire was looped onto the demo recording on 15 Apr to help review, but has never engaged - an unopened second thread.

gaps: Economic buyer / budget owner never named or engaged (knowledge gap; Scott must 'beg' for funds).
